VAL_METRIC: 0.5858181818181819
analysis: Tested adding multiple dropout layers within the `Simple3DCNN` to further combat overfitting during the BraTS fine-tuning stage. Placed `Dropout(0.3)` after the 3rd pool layer and `Dropout(0.4)` after the 4th pool. While training stabilized, the maximum peak validation score across 10 folds reached 0.5858. This indicates that excessive dropout actually disrupts the delicate transfer of the spatially embedded pre-trained geometric priors. Standard data augmentation (as in Iteration 7) is a more effective regularization mechanism than structural dropout for this specific transfer learning curriculum.
